title: manulea dregei
intro: Manulea dregei is a plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the "Wild Dagga" due to its resemblance to the cultivated form of Leonotis leonurus. Traditionally, Manulea dregei has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Manulea dregei is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety are not extensively scientifically documented.
